# Athlete Dossier

## Identity
- **Name:** Edoardo
- **Sport(s):** Road cycling, running, ski mountaineering, climbing
- **Primary discipline:** Multisport endurance

## Physiology & Zones

### Heart Rate
- **Resting HR:** 45 bpm
- **Max HR:** 185 bpm
- **LTHR (running):** 171 bpm
- **LTHR (cycling):** 165 bpm

### Power
- **FTP:** No power meter — HR-based analysis only
- **Zone preference:** run:hr, cycling:hr

### Training Zones (HR-based, 5-zone Coggan)
#### Running (LTHR 171)
- Z1 (Recovery): < 145 bpm
- Z2 (Endurance): 145–156 bpm
- Z3 (Tempo): 156–163 bpm
- Z4 (Threshold): 163–171 bpm
- Z5 (VO2max): > 171 bpm

#### Cycling (LTHR 165)
- Z1 (Recovery): < 140 bpm
- Z2 (Endurance): 140–151 bpm
- Z3 (Tempo): 151–158 bpm
- Z4 (Threshold): 158–165 bpm
- Z5 (VO2max): > 165 bpm

## Training Background
- **Years training:** 4 years climbing (continuous), 5 years ski mountaineering, 2 months running + road cycling
- **Weekly volume:** ~8.5h/week (run + cycling, excluding climbing)
- **Current phase:** Base building

## Goals & Race Calendar
| Date | Event | Priority |
|------|-------|----------|
| 29 Nov 2026 | Turin Half Marathon | A |
| Winter 2026/27 | Ski mountaineering races (TBD) | B |
| 17 Apr 2027 | Mezzalama | A |

## Health & Limitations
- No current injuries or physical limitations
- Occasional mild left ankle discomfort (minor sprain ~4 weeks ago) — monitor under load
- No chronic conditions

## Preferences
- **Structure:** Structured training plans preferred
- **Rest day:** Tuesday (off-limits for hard sessions)
- **Environment:** Outdoor preferred
- **Indoor:** Acceptable when necessary

## Notes
- Strong aerobic base from climbing and ski mountaineering background
- Running and cycling are relatively new (2 months) — musculoskeletal adaptation still ongoing, especially with ankle history
- Mezzalama requires specific ski mountaineering volume from autumn 2026 onward — periodization should account for this transition
- Climbing volume not tracked in Intervals.icu but contributes to overall fatigue
